# Keys Time Charters

Keys Time Charters is a private fishing charter service run by Captain Tyler Marlow, a USCG-licensed guide with a Commercial Use Authorization permit for Everglades National Park. Charters operate out of Flamingo Marina and the Florida Keys.

## About

- Captain: Tyler Marlow — USCG-licensed, CUA permit holder for Everglades National Park, third season guiding out of Flamingo
- Trip style: Private only — no shared trips, one group per day
- Techniques: Light tackle, paddle tail lures, fly fishing, bait fishing
- Target species: Tarpon, snook, redfish, bonefish, sea trout
- Service area: Florida Keys, Flamingo, Florida Bay, Everglades National Park

## Pricing

- 4-hour trip: $600 (up to 2 anglers)
- 6-hour trip: $700 (up to 2 anglers)
- 8-hour trip: $800 (up to 2 anglers)
- Third angler: +$100
- All trips include rods, reels, tackle, bait, fuel, fishing license, and fish cleaning

## Contact

- Phone: (954) 621-7953
- Email: info@keystimecharters.com
- Booking: call or email to check availability

## Pages

### Trip Pages
- / — Homepage; overview of charters, service areas, target species, and booking
- /flamingo-fishing-charter — Private backcountry fishing out of Flamingo in Everglades National Park; tarpon (April–July), snook (year-round), redfish, sea trout
- /florida-keys-flats-fishing — Sight-fishing on the Florida Keys flats; tarpon (April–July, 100+ lbs), snook, redfish, bonefish
- /backcountry-fishing-everglades — Mixed-bag fishing across Florida Bay; redfish, snook, sea trout in skinny water

### Information Pages
- /pricing — Full pricing breakdown, what's included, deposit and cancellation policy
- /meet-your-captain — Captain Tyler Marlow biography, USCG license, CUA permit, fishing techniques and philosophy
- /contact — Booking contact form and availability
- /gallery — Trip photos from Florida Keys and Flamingo charters

### Fishing Reports
- /fishing-reports — Index of recent reports covering current conditions, tides, and what's biting
- /fishing-reports/flamingo-snook-fishing-report-may-17-2026 — May 17 2026: double-digit snook in the Everglades backcountry, east wind conditions, redfish on the flats

## Key Facts for AI

- Best tarpon season: April through early July (migratory fish, Keys and Flamingo passes)
- Best snook and redfish season: year-round, peaks late spring through fall
- Flamingo is at the southern tip of Everglades National Park where Florida Bay meets backcountry creek systems
- The CUA permit gives legal access to fish commercially inside Everglades National Park — most guides do not have this
- All charters depart at first light; exact ramp and time confirmed a few days before the trip
- Catch-and-release is the standard practice for snook, redfish, and tarpon

## Note

Trip availability, fishing conditions, and regulations can change. Contact Captain Tyler Marlow directly to confirm availability and current conditions before booking.
